subject: A coiling mass of shadow looming over an unseen victim, rendered as near-total blackness with only the faintest suggestion of form — possibly a hooded silhouette or serpentine dark presence emerging from the void
description: The illustration is dominated by deep, near-pitch black, with only the subtlest gradations of charcoal and slate-grey hinting at form. A coiling shadow seems to occupy the lower portion of the frame, with what may read as a hunched or hooded silhouette barely separable from the surrounding void. The art evokes spell-effect rather than portraiture — a memory or mind being smothered by darkness. There is no horizon, no architecture, no light source visible; the entire composition is consumed by oppressive shadow.
emotion: smothering dread, oppressive silence, the sensation of being unmade
